entry.title: Sort an Array of Hashes in Ruby
entry.content:
Let say we have and array of hashes like this ```ruby Array of hashes

list=[

  {:name => 'John', :age =>40},
  {:name => 'Ana', :age =>25},
  {:name => 'Peter', :age =>30},
 ]

```

and we need to sort of one of the field of the hash.

We use the method sort_by! from the Array class ```ruby sort_by method http://ruby-doc.org/core-2.0/Array.html#method-i-sort_by-21 list.sort_by{|item| item[:age]}

=> [{“name”=>“Ana”, “age”=>25}, {“name”=>“Peter”, “age”=>30}, {“name”=>“John”, “age”=>40}]

```

If we want to sort in descinding order we add a ‘–’ before the field ```ruby sort_by method descinding list.sort_by{|item| -item[:age]}

=> [{“name”=>“John”, “age”=>40}, {“name”=>“Peter”, “age”=>30}, {“name”=>“Ana”, “age”=>25}]

```
